titles.en: Chapter 23 – The Rubies and the Emeralds
Chapter 23 – The Rubies and the Emeralds

segments: كَانَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْيَوَاقِيتِ فَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ .
arabic.text: كَانَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْيَوَاقِيتِ فَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, form his father, from Ali Bin Ma’bad, from Al Husayn Bin Khalid,
(It has been narrated) from Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘You should be wearing rings with the rubies for these negate the poverty’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, form his father, from Ali Bin Ma’bad, from Al Husayn Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘You should be wearing rings with the rubies for these negate the poverty’.

segments: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ali ibn Ma’bad from al-Husayn ibn Khalid who has said the following: “Al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say, ‘You must use ruby as a ring, it disallows poverty.’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ali ibn Ma’bad from al-Husayn ibn Khalid who has said the following: “Al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say, ‘You must use ruby as a ring, it disallows poverty.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 23 – The Rubies and the Emeralds

segments: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْيَوَاقِيتِ فَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ .
arabic.text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْيَوَاقِيتِ فَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahma Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Muhammad Bin Al Fazeyl, from Abu Al Hassan, from his father, from his grandfather who said,
‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘You should be wearing a ring with the rubies and these negate the poverty’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Ahma Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Muhammad Bin Al Fazeyl, from Abu Al Hassan, from his father, from his grandfather who said,

‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘You should be wearing a ring with the rubies and these negate the poverty’.

segments: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl from abu al-Hassan from his father from his grandfather who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, ‘You must use rubies as rings; they disallow poverty.’”
en.sarwar.text: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl from abu al-Hassan from his father from his grandfather who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, ‘You must use rubies as rings; they disallow poverty.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ ( عليهم السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 23 – The Rubies and the Emeralds

segments: قَالَ لِي يَوْماً وَ أَمْلَى عَلَيَّ مِنْ كِتَابٍ التَّخَتُّمُ بِالزُّمُرُّدِ يُسْرٌ لَا عُسْرَ فِيهِ .
arabic.text: قَالَ لِي يَوْماً وَ أَمْلَى عَلَيَّ مِنْ كِتَابٍ التَّخَتُّمُ بِالزُّمُرُّدِ يُسْرٌ لَا عُسْرَ فِيهِ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from a man from our companions and he was Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazl, and he had the epitaph of Sikbaj,

(It has been narrated) from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr the owner of the hostels, and he used to stand for one of the affairs of Al-Maazy<sup>asws</sup> (7<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) who said, ‘One day he<sup>asws</sup> said to me and dictated to me from a book: ‘Wear a ring of the emerald, there is ease, and there is no difficulties in it’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from a man from our companions and he was Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazl, and he had the epitaph of Sikbaj,

(It has been narrated) from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr the owner of the hostels, and he used to stand for one of the affairs of Al-Maazy<sup>asws</sup> (7<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) who said, ‘One day he<sup>asws</sup> said to me and dictated to me from a book: ‘Wear a ring of the emerald, there is ease, and there is no difficulties in it’.

segments: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Harun ibn Muslim from a man from a certain persons of our people, al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn al-Fadl called Sikbaj from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr, Sahib al-’Anzal who worked for certain tasks of al-Madiy, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said the following: “He (the Imam), Alayhi al-Salam, one day said to me dictating in a book, ‘Using emerald in a ring is a comfort which is free of difficulties.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Harun ibn Muslim from a man from a certain persons of our people, al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn al-Fadl called Sikbaj from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr, Sahib al-’Anzal who worked for certain tasks of al-Madiy, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said the following: “He (the Imam), Alayhi al-Salam, one day said to me dictating in a book, ‘Using emerald in a ring is a comfort which is free of difficulties.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/359)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/359)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا وَ هُوَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْفَضْلِ وَ يُلَقَّبُ سِكْبَاجَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ صَاحِبِ الْأَنْزَالِ وَ كَانَ يَقُومُ بِبَعْضِ أُمُورِ الْمَاضِي ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

parts.text: هُوَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْفَضْلِ
أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ صَاحِبِ الْأَنْزَالِ
كَانَ يَقُومُ بِبَعْضِ أُمُورِ الْمَاضِي ( عليه السلام )

titles.en: Chapter 23 – The Rubies and the Emeralds

segments: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْيَوَاقِيتِ فَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ .
arabic.text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْيَوَاقِيتِ فَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الْفَقْرَ .

segments: Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Dahqan Ubeydullah, from Al Husayn Bin Khalid,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘You should be wearing rings with the emeralds, for these negate the poverty’.
en.hubeali.text:
Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Dahqan Ubeydullah, from Al Husayn Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘You should be wearing rings with the emeralds, for these negate the poverty’.

segments: 4. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Dihqan ‘Ubayd Allah from al-Husayn ibn Khalid who has said the following: “I once heard abu al-Hassan, Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘You must use ruby in the ring; it disallows poverty.’”
en.sarwar.text: 4. Sahl ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Dihqan ‘Ubayd Allah from al-Husayn ibn Khalid who has said the following: “I once heard abu al-Hassan, Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘You must use ruby in the ring; it disallows poverty.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/359)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/359)

narratorChain.raw: سَهْلُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنِ الدِّهْقَانِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 23 – The Rubies and the Emeralds

segments: يُسْتَحَبُّ التَّخَتُّمُ بِالْيَاقُوتِ .
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Usman Bin Isa, from Bakr Bin Muhammad,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is desirable, the wearing of the ring with the ruby’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Usman Bin Isa, from Bakr Bin Muhammad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is desirable, the wearing of the ring with the ruby’.

segments: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Bakr ibn Muhammad who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is Mustahab (desirable) to use ruby as a ring.’”
en.sarwar.text: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Bakr ibn Muhammad who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is Mustahab (desirable) to use ruby as a ring.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)
gradings.label: حسن أو موثق
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/359)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ بَكْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ
